prose:
Frontier-orbital calculation on benzene (12 atoms: 6 C + 6 H) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard'), gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1, in PySCF via the chemkit frontier-orbitals skill. The geometry was taken as-is from the supplied .xyz file (single-point; no re-optimization). SCF converged in 8 cycles. Integrity status: trustworthy=true, all checks passed. No warnings reported.

HOMO energy: −7.0468329 eV (orbital index 20, occupation 2.0)
LUMO energy: −0.41574728 eV (orbital index 21, occupation 0.0)
HOMO–LUMO gap: 6.6310857 eV (headline value)

The near-degeneracy of HOMO/HOMO-1 (−7.0468 vs −7.0473 eV) and LUMO/LUMO+1 (−0.4157 vs −0.4151 eV) correctly reflects benzene's D6h symmetry (e1g and e2u degenerate pairs). These are Koopmans-theorem orbital energies, not excitation energies.
